data.title: Brookfield to invest up to SEK 95B in Swedish AI infrastructure
data.description:
Brookfield (NYSE:BN) (TSX:BN:CA) announced on Wednesday an up to SEK 95B investment in a Swedish artificial intelligence, or AI, infrastructure, representing one of the largest AI investments by the alternative asset manager in Europe.

The investment extends the company's partnership with the Swedish government, its public authorities, academia, and businesses in the region.

The investment will be centered on a new large AI center in Strängnäs, and Brookfield is set to sign a land allocation agreement for c.350K sqm of additional land. The additions will enable the data center site to more than double its capacity from 300MW to 750MW.

data.title: Wedbush launches ETF focused on AI
data.description:
After announcing it was getting into the exchange traded fund industry in February, asset manager Wedbush Securities has launched a new ETF focused on artificial intelligence.

The ticker? Perhaps no surprise that it's “IVES.”

Known as the Dan Ives Wedbush AI Revolution ETF, the fund will be composed of 30 companies under the purview of the well-known and often loquacious tech analyst. Notable names include Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) and others.

“It's based on our research,” Ives told CNBC. “So as new companies come in, then some companies could come out. This is a living organism, in terms of this AI 30. It's not static. And that's a key part of the theme here, because the theme will continue to evolve.”

Ives, who was interviewed by Seeking Alpha in January 2024, initially launched the list of the AI 30 companies last month. 2025 has been an inflection year within enterprise generative AI as true adoption has gone from idea to scale, with more companies looking to invest in AI to cut costs and boost productivity, Ives said last month. 

“It's all about the use cases exploding which is driving this tech transformation being led by software and chips into the rest of 2025 and beyond and thus speaks to our tech bull and AI Revolution thesis with these 30 core names,” Ives said last month. 

The management fee on the IVES ETF is 0.75%.

data.title: New Century Logistics proposes 51% acquisition of Asiatic Logistics
data.description:
New Century Logistics (NASDAQ:NCEW) has signed a Letter of Intent with Asiatic's sole shareholder, Chiu Nga Ting, to acquire 51% of Asiatic's shares.

The move aims to boost New Century's presence in Asia and improve its global logistics ecosystem.

The LOI signifies a significant milestone in the partnership, highlighting the mutual commitment to optimizing operational efficiencies and synergies.

New Century and Asiatic signed a Strategic Cooperation Framework Agreement in July 2023 to enhance transportation services and share resources. Currently, acquiring 51% of Asiatic aims to strengthen their partnership and achieve mutual benefits.

The acquisition is expected to strengthen New Century's Asian market advantages and upgrade its global logistics network.

The proposed deal offers strategic value to New Century in three main areas: it will enhance logistics in Asia, expand global networks between Asia, Europe, and the Middle East by combining operations, and improve resource sharing for better efficiency and market reach.

New Century plans to use Japan as a key hub to expand its business in Asia after completing an acquisition. The company aims to strengthen Japan's role as a "transshipment center" that links Southeast Asia, Europe, America, and the Middle East through logistics channels. Other key initiatives includes: e-commerce services, resource integration, and digital upgrades for business growth in Asia and beyond.

data.title: Vigil Neuroscience fails in mid-stage study for lead asset
data.description:
• Vigil Neuroscience (NASDAQ:VIGL) announced on Wednesday that a mid-stage trial for its lead candidate, iluzanebart, didn’t succeed in patients with a rare neurological disease called adult-onset leukoencephalopathy with axonal spheroids and pigmented glia (ALSP).
  • The Watertown, Massachusetts-based biotech, which recently agreed to be acquired by Sanofi (SNY), said that based on the results, the company has decided to discontinue a Phase 2 long-term extension study for iluzanebart, also known as VGL101.
  • While the experimental therapy failed to achieve beneficial effects on biomarker or clinical efficacy endpoints in the open-label trial named IGNITE, Vigil (NASDAQ:VIGL) said that the drug at 20 mg/kg and 40 mg/kg doses indicated a favorable safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetic profile.
  • Sanofi’s (GCVRZ) $470M deal in May to acquire Vigil (NASDAQ:VIGL) didn’t include iluzanebart.

data.title: European indexes in green despite Trump's 50% tariff on metals
data.description:
Update: The revised post updates market action as of 7.20am ET.

London (UKX) +0.19% to 8,803, hovering near the flat line for a third straight session on Wednesday. UK composite, services PMI rise in May.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.62% to 24,243, Germany's service sector records steepest drop in over two years.

France (CAC:IND) +0.59% to 7,809, French service sector downturn eases in May.

Spain's service sector growth falls to 18-month low in May. Spain's industrial production rose by 0.6% Y/Y in April.

The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic rose to 2.4% in May. Retail sales in the Czech Republic climbed by 5.8% Y/Y in April.

Industrial production in Georgia rose by 2.4% Y/Y in Q1.

Sweden’s Services PMI rose to 50.8 in May.

Euro Area composite PMI eases to 50.20 points in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) gained 0.38% to 550.5, with investors apparently looking past the latest trade developments. Optimism over US-EU trade negotiations and anticipated ECB rate cuts boosted the demand for tech stocks. Notable gains came from STMicroelectronics (STM) and Infineon Technologies (OTCQX:IFNNY) (OTCQX:IFNNF), while steel-related stocks also advanced. Despite the geopolitical backdrop and Trump's tariffs on metals, market focus remains on regional economic data.

Coming up in the next session: Investors will keenly watch at ECB interest rate decision due this week.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was almost flat at 4.46%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points at 2.52%.

UK's 10-year yield was flat 4.65%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Helius Medical Technologies now compliant with Nasdaq minimum price rule
data.description:
• Helius Medical Technologies (NASDAQ:HSDT) received formal notice from Nasdaq indicating that the company has evidenced full compliance with the minimum bid price requirement set forth earlier.
  • The hearing panel further reminded the company that it is also required to regain compliance with the equity requirement rule by June 30, 2025, under the extension previously granted
  • The hearing process will remain open until the company has demonstrated compliance with alllisting requirements.

data.title: Main Street invests $45.4M in aerial survey and geospatial services provider
data.description:
Principal investment firm Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) announced on Wednesday a new $45.4M portfolio investment in an "aerial survey and geospatial services provider".

Shares were +0.43% pre-market to $56.70.

The company partnered with its existing investors to facilitate the minority recapitalization with a combination of first lien, senior secured term debt, and a direct equity investment.

Additionally, Main Street provided the aerial survey platform with a revolving line of credit.

data.title: UnitedHealth approves cash dividend of $2.21 per share
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) Board authorized payment of a cash dividend of $2.21 per share, to be paid June 24, to common stock shareholders of record as of the close of business June 16.

Shareholders re-elected all current directors: Charles Baker, Timothy Flynn, Paul Garcia, Kristen Gil, Stephen Hemsley, Michele Hooper, F. William McNabb III, Valerie Montgomery Rice, M.D. and John Noseworthy, M.D.

data.title: Dollar Tree falls after warning about earnings volatility amid the tariff backdrop
data.description:
Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) reported sales from continuing operations were up 11.3% to $4.64 billion in Q1. Same-store net sales growth was +5.4% for the Dollar Tree banner, driven by a 2.5% increase in traffic and a 2.8% increase in average ticket.

Gross profit increased 11.7% to $1.6 billion, and the gross margin rate expanded 20 basis points to 35.6%. The expansion in gross margin was driven primarily by lower freight, improved mark-on and lower occupancy costs due to sales leverage, partially offset by increased distribution, shrink, and markdown costs. Adjusted operating income increased 1.4% to $387.8 million, and adjusted operating margin contracted 80 basis points to 8.4%. EPS from continuing operations was reported at $1.26 vs. $1.21 consensus.

The company generated $379 million of net cash provided by operating activities from continuing operations and $130 million of free cash flow from continuing operations. At the end of the quarter, Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) had approximately $519.7 million remaining under its $2.5 billion share repurchase authorization, $1.0 billion of cash and cash equivalents, no borrowings under its revolvers and no commercial paper outstanding.

Of note, the results of Family Dollar were presented in Dollar Tree's (DLTR) financial results as discontinued operations due to the pending sale.

Dollar Tree (DLTR) added 148 stores to its base during the quarter.

CEO Mike Creedon said the strong first quarter performance underscores the progress the retailer has made against strategic priorities and is a clear signal that customers are responding positively to the changes we are making. "History has shown that we have the resilience to emerge stronger from periods of economic uncertainty and in today's rapidly evolving environment, we see a meaningful opportunity to further elevate the value, convenience, and discovery that our customers depend on Dollar Tree to provide," he added.

Looking ahead, Dollar Tree (DLTR) said given trends to date and expectations for the balance of the quarter, the company believes second quarter comparable net sales growth will be towards the higher end of its full-year outlook range of 3% to 5%. Dollar Tree (DLTR) also guided for full-year EPS of $5.15 to $5.65 (midpoint $5.35) vs. $5.28 consensus and a prior outlook for $5.00 to $5.50.

"Over the balance of fiscal 2025, the company expects to mitigate the earnings impact of the cost pressures it faces, including higher tariffs. In the near-term, it does expect to see some earnings volatility based on the timing of the various inputs and outputs to the company’s financial results."

Shares of Dollar Tree (DLTR) fell 3.9% in premarket trading to $92.92. Short interest on DLTR stands at 7.00% of the total float.

data.title: Chart Industries, Flowserve to combine in all-stock merger
data.description:
Chart Industries (NYSE:GTLS) and Flowserve (NYSE:FLS) said Wednesday they agreed to combine in an all-stock merger valued at ~$19B including debt, creating a differentiated leader in industrial process technologies; Chart -2.8% and Flowserve -2.5% pre-market.

The combined company generated $8.8B in net revenue on a combined LTM basis as of the end of Q1 2025, including $3.7B in aftermarket services revenue.

Under the deal terms, Chart (NYSE:GTLS) shareholders will receive 3.165 shares of Flowserve (NYSE:FLS) common stock for each share of Chart common stock owned; at the close of the transaction, Chart shareholders would own 53.5% and Flowserve shareholders would own 46.5% of the combined company.

Flowserve (FLS) CEO Scott Rowe will serve as the combined company's CEO, while Chart (GTLS) CEO Jill Evanko will become the board chair.

The companies said they expect the combination will generate ~$300M of annual cost synergies within three years, primarily from materials and procurement savings, roofline consolidation, organizational efficiencies, and elimination of duplicate public company costs.

The companies also expect to deliver commercial revenue synergies over time representing at least an incremental 2% growth on the combined company's revenue.
